headline: Le Pen condemns 'welfare dependency,' marking ideological about-face
description: In a speech on May 1, the far-right leader repeatedly used a term she had previously deemed discriminatory toward working-class people.

headline: French prosecutors open criminal investigation into Elon Musk and X
WHO warns of more hantavirus cases in 'limited' outbreak
Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
description: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

headline: France deploys aircraft carrier to Gulf of Aden, ready to act in Strait of Hormuz
description: The French initiative is part of a mission launched at the end of April in cooperation with the UK to unite around 50 countries seeking to peacefully restore passage in the region. The Elysée stated that any action would remain 'at a distance from the belligerents.'

headline: Moscow's Bauman University, the secretive school training Russian hackers
description: According to 2,000 documents obtained by a consortium of international media, including Le Monde, the prestigious Bauman Moscow State Technical University has been discreetly training Russian military intelligence officers, particularly hackers.

headline: Russia, Israel, United States: 'Pariah' pavilions spark controversy at Venice Biennale
description: The major international art event, which this year reopened its doors to Russia, is facing widespread protests. On the ground, artists are divided.

Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
description: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

headline: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
description: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

headline: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.
